blocks.text: Lisa Brown, Chief Operating Officer, {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}}
Re: Controls Retrofit Commissioning
Dear Ms. Brown:

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} has engaged Torchlake Engineering Group LLC to commission the building automation retrofit now finishing installation at your headquarters building. Our role is owner's-side verification: the controls contractor demonstrates, we test, and nothing gets signed off because the graphics look right.
Scope of commissioning:

items: Point-to-point checkout verification on a sampled basis, with full retest of any panel that fails its sample.
Functional performance tests of every air handler, the chilled-water plant, and the emergency sequences.
Two weeks of trend-data review after acceptance, plus opposite-season verification within the term of this engagement.

blocks.text: Jeff Fowler, Engineering Manager, directs the engagement; John Johnson, Project Engineer, runs the functional tests and keeps the issues log your team and the contractor will work from. We will need controls submittals, the points list, and a contractor technician on site during test days.

Commissioning begins on {{fact:f:E-2023-001.start}}. Our fee for the scope above is a fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2023-001.fee}}, invoiced monthly against progress. Scope changes, including retest days beyond the two allowed for contractor failures, are agreed in writing before the work.
We appreciate the continued confidence. Please sign below and return one copy to my attention.
Sincerely,
To: Commissioning team; cc Lisa Brown, Chief Operating Officer, {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}}
From: Jeff Fowler, Engineering Manager
Re: Kickoff, Controls Retrofit Commissioning
Commissioning starts {{fact:f:E-2023-001.start}}. The contractor's checkout is reportedly ninety percent complete, which historically means seventy. Plan test days accordingly.
Assignments:

items: 1. Functional performance tests and the issues log: John Johnson owns both. Every issue gets a number, a location, and a retest date; the log is the single source of truth with the contractor.
2. Test scripts: John drafts from the sequences of operations, Jeff reviews before anything is executed with the contractor watching.
3. Trend review: two-week post-acceptance trends land in the engagement folder weekly; flag anything that hunts, short-cycles, or never reaches setpoint.

blocks.text: Ground rules with the contractor: professional, specific, in writing. We fail tests, not people. Ms. Brown's office gets the issues log summary every Friday; no surprises in the monthly status report.
Next steps: John requests the points list and submittals today. First sampled point-to-point checkout is scheduled once the contractor confirms panel completion in writing.
Commissioning Progress Minutes
Progress meeting held by video with {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} to review functional testing results and the open issues log. Minutes prepared by John Johnson.
Attendees:

items: Jeff Fowler, Engineering Manager, Torchlake Engineering Group
John Johnson, Project Engineer, Torchlake Engineering Group

blocks.text: Discussion. John reported functional testing complete on six of eight air handlers: two passed clean, four passed with minor issues now closed, and two remain blocked by unfinished contractor checkout on the top floors. The chilled-water plant sequence passed its first full test, including the failover to the standby pump. Ms. Brown asked whether the blocked units threaten the seasonal verification window; Jeff confirmed the schedule holds if checkout completes within two weeks, and the contractor has committed to that date in writing. Trend review has flagged one zone that never reaches setpoint overnight; issue 41 in the log, suspected damper actuator, contractor investigating.
Decisions. The two blocked air handlers are scheduled as a single combined test day. Issue 41 escalates to the contractor's project manager if not resolved by the next meeting.

rows: Confirm contractor checkout completion for top floors
Execute combined test day for blocked air handlers
Provide updated occupancy schedule for trend review

blocks.text: Status Report: Controls Retrofit Commissioning

Status: on track, one watch item. Functional testing for {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} is complete across all eight air handlers and the chilled-water plant; the issues log stands at 47 raised, 44 closed. Seasonal verification remains scheduled inside the engagement term. Prepared by Jeff Fowler.
Completed this period

items: Closed functional testing on the final two air handlers after the combined test day, including the retest allowance.
Completed the two-week post-acceptance trend review; John Johnson's issues log drove three contractor fixes, all verified in trends.
Issue 41 (overnight setpoint) closed: failed damper actuator replaced and verified across five consecutive nights.

blocks.text: Watch item
Three issues remain open, all documentation: as-built sequences and the point database export. None block occupancy or the seasonal test, but final acceptance should not be signed until they close.
Budget

The engagement remains within the agreed fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2023-001.fee}}, invoiced monthly per the engagement letter. Both allowed retest days were used; no additional scope has been requested.
